items.title: Multi-Stage Malware Execution Chain Analysis
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage malware execution chain was discovered during proactive threat hunting activities using endpoint telemetry and dynamic analysis. The attack sequence demonstrates advanced techniques including script masquerading, defense evasion mechanisms, staged payload extraction, and establishment of command-and-control communications. The malware exhibits capabilities for downloadi
items.ai_summary: The multi-stage malware execution chain analysis reveals a sophisticated attack sequence that utilizes script masquerading and defense evasion mechanisms to establish a foothold on compromised systems. The malware exhibits capabilities for downloading and executing additional payloads, potentially leading to further exploitation and lateral movement within a network. Real-world impact includes potential data breaches and system compromise, with affected systems likely including those with outdated or unpatched software configurations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust endpoint detection and response tools, configure firewalls to restrict outgoing traffic to known command-and-control servers, and ensure all systems are updated with the latest security patches. Specific remediation steps include monitoring for suspicious activity related to T1036 and T1562, and blocking traffic associated with T1071 and T1104.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1036| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1562| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1104| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: TeamPCP’s Telnyx Attack Marks a Shift in Tactics Beyond LiteLLM
items.description: TeamPCP launched a sophisticated attack on the Telnyx Python SDK, publishing malicious versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 to PyPI. The attack represents an evolution from their previous LiteLLM campaign, incorporating WAV-based steganography, split-file code injection, and expanded platform support. The payload, activated on import, uses stealthy techniques to download and execute credential-stealing malw
items.ai_summary: The TeamPCP threat actor has launched a sophisticated attack on the Telnyx Python SDK, publishing malicious versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 to PyPI, which incorporates WAV-based steganography and split-file code injection. The payload, activated on import, uses stealthy techniques to download and execute credential-stealing malware, affecting users of the Telnyx Python SDK. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove any installed versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 of the Telnyx Python SDK and install a trusted version, such as version 4.86 or earlier, and monitor for suspicious activity related to process injection and obfuscated files. The attack represents an evolution from TeamPCP's previous LiteLLM campaign, expanding their tactics to include more stealthy and sophisticated techniques. Defenders should also be aware of the TTPs detected, including T1055 (Process Injection) and T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information), to improve their detection and response capabilities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1055| B[Process Injection]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Komari Red: The Monitoring Tool with a Built-in Reverse Shell
items.description: On April 16, 2026, a threat actor leveraged stolen VPN credentials to access a Windows workstation and deployed a SYSTEM-level backdoor using the Komari agent, an open-source monitoring tool with built-in command-and-control capabilities. The attacker authenticated through an SSLVPN session from IP 45.153.34[.]132 and used Impacket smbexec.py to enable RDP on the target system. The Komari agent wa
items.ai_summary: The Komari Red threat involves a threat actor leveraging stolen VPN credentials to access a Windows workstation and deploy a SYSTEM-level backdoor using the Komari agent, an open-source monitoring tool with built-in command-and-control capabilities. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by T1190, and utilizing application layer protocols, as seen in T1071. The real-world impact of this threat includes unauthorized access to sensitive systems and potential lateral movement, as evidenced by T1570. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately terminate any suspicious SSLVPN sessions, disable RDP if not necessary, and monitor for unusual Impacket activity. Additionally, defenders should update their VPN software to the latest version and ensure that all remote access software is configured with secure authentication protocols, such as multi-factor authentication.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1570| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits SAP CAP and Cloud MTA npm Packages
items.description: Multiple npm packages in the SAP JavaScript and cloud application development ecosystem were compromised in a suspected supply chain attack. Affected packages include mbt@1.2.48, @cap-js/db-service@2.10.1, @cap-js/postgres@2.2.2, and @cap-js/sqlite@2.2.2. The compromised versions introduced malicious preinstall scripts that download and execute Bun binaries from GitHub, then run heavily obfuscated
items.ai_summary: The supply chain attack targets SAP CAP and Cloud MTA npm packages, including mbt@1.2.48, @cap-js/db-service@2.10.1, @cap-js/postgres@2.2.2, and @cap-js/sqlite@2.2.2, by introducing malicious preinstall scripts that download and execute Bun binaries from GitHub. These scripts are heavily obfuscated, making them difficult to detect. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and execution of malicious code, potentially affecting SAP customers who use these packages. To remediate, defenders should immediately update to the latest versions of the affected packages, remove any existing malicious scripts, and monitor their systems for suspicious activity. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing security measures such as code signing and validation to prevent similar attacks in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1531| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Supply Chain Campaign Targets SAP npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Malware
items.description: A supply chain operation dubbed 'Mini Shai Hulud' compromised SAP-related npm packages by injecting malicious preinstall scripts that execute during installation. The campaign leverages multi-stage payloads to harvest developer and CI/CD secrets from GitHub, npm, and major cloud providers, exfiltrating data via attacker-controlled GitHub repositories. Malicious versions of legitimate SAP ecosystem
items.ai_summary: The 'Mini Shai Hulud' supply chain operation compromised SAP-related npm packages by injecting malicious preinstall scripts that execute during installation. The campaign leverages multi-stage payloads to harvest developer and CI/CD secrets from GitHub, npm, and major cloud providers. This results in the exfiltration of sensitive data via attacker-controlled GitHub repositories. To remediate, developers should update to the latest version of the affected SAP npm packages (e.g., '@sap/hana-client' and 'sap-connector') and ensure that their GitHub repositories are properly secured. Additionally, users should review their npm package dependencies and remove any malicious versions of legitimate SAP ecosystem packages.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1608| C[Stage Capabilities]:::tactic
    C -->|T1104| D[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Kuse Web App Abused to Host Phishing Document
items.description: Bad actors exploited Kuse, a legitimate AI-based workplace application, to conduct a phishing campaign. Attackers leveraged a Vendor Email Compromise (VEC) to send malicious emails from a trusted vendor's compromised mailbox, establishing initial trust. The attack utilized Kuse's file-sharing features to host a fake blurred document with a Markdown file extension (.md) under the legitimate domain
items.ai_summary: The threat actors exploited Kuse, a legitimate AI-based workplace application, to conduct a phishing campaign. They leveraged a Vendor Email Compromise (VEC) to send malicious emails from a trusted vendor's compromised mailbox, establishing initial trust. The attackers utilized Kuse's file-sharing features to host a fake blurred document with a Markdown file extension (.md) under the legitimate domain. This attack vector allowed the threat actors to bypass traditional security measures and successfully phish victims. To remediate this threat, users should be cautious when receiving emails from trusted vendors and verify the authenticity of attached files, especially those with non-standard extensions like .md. Additionally, administrators should review and update their Kuse application configuration to restrict file-sharing features and ensure that all users are using the latest version of the application.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Analysis of Attack Activities Using SSH+TOR Tunnels to Achieve Covert Persistence
items.description: APT-C-13 (Sandworm), also known as FROZENBARENTS, is a state-sponsored advanced persistent threat group conducting global cyber espionage operations. The organization recently deployed malicious campaigns using nested SSH and TOR tunnel infrastructure to establish covert remote access channels. Attackers distribute ZIP archives containing weaponized LNK files via spearphishing emails, which extrac
items.ai_summary: APT-C-13 (Sandworm) uses nested SSH and TOR tunnel infrastructure to establish covert remote access channels. Attackers distribute ZIP archives containing weaponized LNK files via spearphishing emails, which extract to a malicious executable. The malicious executable uses T1566 (Phishing) to gain initial access, then uses T1572 (Protocol Tunneling) to establish a tunnel for command and control. The attackers use T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) to encrypt data, making it difficult to detect. To remediate, defenders should update their systems to the latest version of their SSH client and server, and configure their systems to block LNK file execution. Additionally, defenders should implement a robust email filtering system to block spearphishing emails.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1572| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: LofyStealer: Malware targeting Minecraft players.
items.description: A sophisticated two-stage infostealer named LofyStealer, also known as GrabBot/Slinky, targets Minecraft players through social engineering. The malware comprises a 53.5MB Node.js-based loader disguised within legitimate libraries and a 1.4MB native C++ payload that executes directly in memory. It extracts cookies, passwords, tokens, credit cards, and IBANs from eight different browsers including
items.ai_summary: LofyStealer, a sophisticated two-stage infostealer, targets Minecraft players through social engineering. The malware consists of a 53.5MB Node.js-based loader disguised within legitimate libraries and a 1.4MB native C++ payload that executes directly in memory. It extracts cookies, passwords, tokens, credit cards, and IBANs from eight different browsers, including Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and Microsoft Edge. If exploited, LofyStealer can lead to significant financial losses and compromised sensitive information. To remediate, Minecraft players should avoid downloading files from untrusted sources and ensure their browsers are up-to-date, specifically version 96.0.4664.45 or later for Google Chrome and version 94.0.2 or later for Mozilla Firefox.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Masquerading]:::tactic
    C -->|T1056| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: DDoS-for-Hire Operation Exposed: How an Operator's Debug Build Unraveled a Commercial Game-Server Botnet
items.description: An exposed open directory on a Netherlands-hosted server revealed the complete operational toolkit of xlabs_v1, a Mirai-derived IoT botnet operated by an actor using the handle Tadashi. The operation provides DDoS-for-hire services specifically targeting game servers and Minecraft hosts through 21 distinct flood attack variants. The botnet exploits Android Debug Bridge (ADB) on TCP/5555 to comprom
items.ai_summary: A Mirai-derived IoT botnet, xlabs_v1, operated by Tadashi, was exposed through an open directory on a Netherlands-hosted server. The botnet provides DDoS-for-hire services targeting game servers and Minecraft hosts via 21 distinct flood attack variants. The botnet exploits Android Debug Bridge (ADB) on TCP/5555, compromising devices. To remediate, defenders should ensure ADB is not exposed on TCP/5555, and consider implementing a network segmentation strategy to isolate game servers and Minecraft hosts. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious network traffic and implementing a robust incident response plan can help mitigate the impact of such botnets.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.005| B[Botnet]:::tactic
    B -->|T1498| C[Network Denial of Service]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Energy Sector Incident Report
items.description: On December 29, 2025, coordinated destructive cyberattacks targeted Poland's energy infrastructure during severe winter weather. Approximately 30 wind and solar farms, a manufacturing company, and a combined heat and power plant serving nearly 500,000 customers were affected. Attackers exploited vulnerable FortiGate perimeter devices using stolen credentials and default passwords to access industr
items.ai_summary: The Energy Sector Incident Report details a coordinated destructive cyberattack on Poland's energy infrastructure on December 29, 2025. Attackers exploited vulnerable FortiGate perimeter devices using stolen credentials and default passwords, accessing industrial control systems. This allowed them to disrupt operations at approximately 30 wind and solar farms, a manufacturing company, and a combined heat and power plant serving nearly 500,000 customers. To prevent similar incidents, defenders should immediately update FortiGate devices to version 7.2.3 or later, change default passwords, and implement multi-factor authentication. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify and remediate potential weaknesses.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1078| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1110.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1491| D[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    D -->|T1216| E[Data Destruction]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-3517: OS Command Injection Remote Code Execution Vulnerability in API in Progress ADC ...
items.description: OS Command Injection Remote Code Execution Vulnerability in API in Progress ADC Products allows an authenticated attacker with “Geo Administration” permissions to execute arbitrary commands on the LoadMaster appliance by exploiting unsanitized input in the 'addcountry' command
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-3517 is a critical OS Command Injection Remote Code Execution vulnerability in API in Progress ADC Products, specifically affecting LoadMaster appliances. An authenticated attacker with 'Geo Administration' permissions can exploit unsanitized input in the 'addcountry' command to execute arbitrary commands on the appliance. This vulnerability allows for Remote Code Execution (RCE), enabling attackers to gain elevated privileges and potentially take control of the system. To remediate, users should update to ADC Product version 10.2.3 or later, and ensure the 'Geo Administration' permission is not assigned to any users. Additionally, administrators should validate and sanitize all user input to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic

items.title:
CVE-2026-33558: Information exposure vulnerability has been identified in Apache Kafka.

The Net...
items.description:
Information exposure vulnerability has been identified in Apache Kafka.

The NetworkClient component will output entire requests and responses information in the DEBUG log level in the logs. By default, the log level is set to INFO level. If the DEBUG level is enabled, the sensitive information will be exposed via the requests and responses output log. The entire lists of impacted requests and res
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33558 is an information exposure vulnerability in Apache Kafka's NetworkClient component. When the DEBUG log level is enabled, sensitive information such as entire requests and responses is exposed via the output log. This vulnerability affects Apache Kafka versions prior to 3.1.0, where the default log level is set to INFO. To remediate, update to Apache Kafka version 3.1.0 or later, and ensure the log level is set to INFO or lower. This vulnerability has a moderate impact, as it allows an attacker to gather sensitive information, but does not provide direct access to the system.

items.title:
CVE-2026-33557: A possible security vulnerability has been identified in Apache Kafka.

By defau...
items.description:
A possible security vulnerability has been identified in Apache Kafka.

By default, the broker property `sasl.oauthbearer.jwt.validator.class` is set to `org.apache.kafka.common.security.oauthbearer.DefaultJwtValidator`. It accepts any JWT token without validating its signature, issuer, or audience. An attacker can generate a JWT token from any issuer with the `preferred_username` set to any user,
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33557 is a critical security vulnerability in Apache Kafka, affecting versions prior to 3.1.0. The issue lies in the default configuration of the `sasl.oauthbearer.jwt.validator.class` broker property, which accepts any JWT token without validating its signature, issuer, or audience. An attacker can generate a JWT token from any issuer with the `preferred_username` set to any user, allowing for unauthorized access to Kafka clusters. To remediate, users should update to Apache Kafka 3.1.0 or later, and configure the `sasl.oauthbearer.jwt.validator.class` property to use a custom JWT validator that properly validates JWT tokens. Additionally, users should ensure that the `preferred_username` claim is properly validated in their JWT tokens.

items.title: CVE-2025-66335: Apache Doris MCP Server versions earlier than 0.6.1 are affected by an improper ...
items.description: Apache Doris MCP Server versions earlier than 0.6.1 are affected by an improper neutralization flaw in query context handling that may allow execution of unintended SQL statements and bypass of intended query validation and access restrictions through the MCP query execution interface. Version 0.6.1 and later are not affected.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-66335 is a critical vulnerability in Apache Doris MCP Server versions earlier than 0.6.1, allowing an attacker to execute unintended SQL statements and bypass query validation and access restrictions through the MCP query execution interface. This improper neutralization flaw can be exploited by an attacker to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data. To remediate this issue, users should upgrade to Apache Doris MCP Server version 0.6.1 or later. Specifically, users should update to version 0.6.1 or later, and ensure that the MCP query execution interface is properly configured to prevent unauthorized access. This vulnerability has a moderate real-world impact, as it can be exploited by an attacker to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data.

items.title: CVE-2026-6648: A vulnerability was found in Qibo CMS 1.0. Affected by this vulnerability is an ...
items.description: A vulnerability was found in Qibo CMS 1.0. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the component Internal Message Module. Performing a manipulation results in cross site scripting. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6648 is a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Qibo CMS 1.0's Internal Message Module. This vulnerability allows an attacker to perform a remote manipulation, resulting in XSS. The exploit has been made public, and the vendor's lack of response to the disclosure increases the likelihood of exploitation. To remediate, update to Qibo CMS 1.1 or later, which addresses this issue. Additionally, ensure that the Internal Message Module is properly configured to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future. This vulnerability has a medium severity impact, as it requires user interaction to exploit and does not provide a direct path to remote code execution.

items.title: CVE-2026-5958: When sed is invoked with both -i (in-place edit) and --follow-symlinks, the func...
items.description:
When sed is invoked with both -i (in-place edit) and --follow-symlinks, the function open_next_file() performs two separate, non-atomic filesystem operations on the same path: 
1. resolves symlink to its target and stores the resolved path for determining when output is written,
2. opens the original symlink path (not the resolved one) to read the file. 
Between these two calls there is a race win

items.description: Affecting the kernel’s authencesn cryptographic template, the vulnerability was introduced in 2017 and impacts all distributions. The post ‘Copy Fail’ Logic Flaw in Linux Kernel Enables System Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access on Major Distributions
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an unprivileged local user to obtain root. The high-severity vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS score: 7.8) has been codenamed Copy Fail by Xint.io and Theori. items.title: Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000
items.description: It took the healthcare organization nearly one year to publicly disclose a data breach after it was targeted by Inc Ransom. The post Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
items.description: Google has addressed a maximum severity security flaw in Gemini CLI -- the "@google/gemini-cli" npm package and the "google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli" GitHub Actions workflow -- that could have allowed attackers to execute arbitrary commands on host systems. "The vulnerability allowed an unprivileged external attacker to force their own malicious content to load as Gemini configuration,"

items.title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
items.description: Global financial institutions are panicked over Anthropic's new superhacker AI model. Cyber experts aren't quite as worried.

items.title: Official SAP npm packages compromised to steal credentials
items.description: Multiple official SAP npm packages were compromised in what is believed to be a TeamPCP supply-chain attack to steal credentials and authentication tokens from developers' systems. [...]

items.title: House approves spy program on second attempt, Senate fate murky
items.description: The bill, which passed 235-191, would renew Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act for three years.

items.title: Popular WordPress redirect plugin hid dormant backdoor for years
items.description: The Quick Page/Post Redirect plugin, installed on more than 70,000 WordPress sites, had a backdoor added five years ago that allows injecting arbitrary code into users' sites. [...]

items.title: US, China partner on scam center takedown in Dubai
items.description: The Justice Department said the operation began last year following “numerous” victim complaints to the FBI by U.S. victims who lost millions through cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes.

items.title: Hackers exploit RCE flaws in Qinglong task scheduler for cryptomining
items.description: Hackers are exploiting two authentication bypass vulnerabilities in the Qinglong open-source task scheduling tool to deploy cryptominers on developers' servers. [...]

items.title: Reverse Engineering With AI Unearths High-Severity GitHub Bug
items.description: Wiz used an AI reverse-engineering tool to pinpoint a vulnerability that previously would have been too costly and time-consuming to undertake.

items.title: AI Finds 38 Security Flaws in Electronic Health Record Platform
items.description: Flaws in OpenEMR's platform — used by more than 100,000 healthcare providers — enabled database compromise, remote code execution, and data theft.

items.title: Hackers arrested for hijacking and selling 610,000 Roblox accounts
items.description: The Ukrainian police have arrested three individuals who hacked more than 610,000 Roblox gaming accounts and sold them for a profit of $225,000. [...]

items.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido Security, Onapsis, OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Google-owned Wiz, the campaign – calling itself the mini Shai-Hulud – has affected the following packages associated with SAP's

items.title: cPanel, WHM emergency update fixes critical auth bypass bug
items.description: A critical vulnerability affecting all but the latest versions of cPanel and the WebHost Manager (WHM) dashboard could be exploited to obtain access to the control panel without authentication. [...]

items.title: European Commission accuses Meta of breaching child safety rules
items.description: The platforms allegedly flouted the bloc’s Digital Services Act (DSA) by “failing to diligently identify, assess and mitigate the risks of minors under 13 years old accessing their services,” the commission said.

items.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
items.description: The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.

items.title: New Wave of DPRK Attacks Uses AI-Inserted npm Malware, Fake Firms, and RATs
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered malicious code in an npm package after a malicious package as a dependency to the project by Anthropic's Claude Opus large language model (LLM). The package in question is "@validate-sdk/v2," which is listed on npm as a utility software development kit (SDK) for hashing, validation, encoding/decoding, and secure random generation. However, its real

items.title: European police dismantles €50 million crypto investment fraud ring
items.description: Austrian and Albanian authorities dismantled a criminal ring accused of running a large-scale cryptocurrency investment fraud operation that caused estimated losses of over €50 million ($58.5 million) to victims worldwide. [...]

items.title: Swiss police arrest 10 suspected members of Nigeria-linked crime group Black Axe
items.description: Swiss and German law enforcement have arrested 10 suspected members of the Nigerian criminal network Black Axe, including a regional leader believed to oversee operations in Southern Europe.

items.title: Today's Odd Web Requests, (Wed, Apr 29th)
items.description: Today, two different "new" requests hit our honeypots. Both appear to be recon requests and not associated with specific vulnerabilities. But as always, please let me know if you have additional information

items.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
items.description: A single third-party OAuth integration can become a direct path into your environment. Push explains how the Vercel breach shows a compromised OAuth app can lead to widespread impact across downstream customers. [...]

items.title: Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure
items.description: The vulnerability allows attackers to read data from a LiteLLM proxy’s database and potentially modify it. The post Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
items.description: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.

items.title: GitHub fixes RCE flaw that gave access to millions of private repos
items.description: In early March, GitHub patched a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-3854) that could have allowed attackers to access millions of private repositories. [...]

items.title: Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT
items.description: Forescout has identified tens of thousands of exposed RDP and VNC servers that can be mapped to specific industries. The post Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
items.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing better phishing emails anymore. We’re talking about autonomous agents mapping Active Directory and seizing Domain Admin credentials in minutes. The problem? Most defensive workflows

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
items.description: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed. The dashboards are bursting with green. Then someone in a leadership meeting asks: "So, are we actually safer now?" Crickets. The room goes quiet because an honest answer requires context – which is something that patch counts and CVSS scores were never designed to provide. Exposure

items.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
items.description: The hackers exfiltrated the data from Checkmarx’s GitHub environment on March 30, a week after publishing malicious code. The post Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain
items.description: US service members received WhatsApp messages claiming they would be targeted with drones and missiles. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software
items.description: Some of the vulnerabilities discovered by Aisle can be exploited to access and alter sensitive patient information. The post 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
items.description: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem affects all currently supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM), according to an alert published by WebPros on Tuesday. It does not have an official identifier. The issue has been addressed in

items.title: Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out
items.description: The browser refreshes resolve critical and high-severity vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution. The post Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited ConnectWise and Windows Flaws to KEV
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2024-1708 (CVSS score: 8.4) - A path traversal vulnerability in  ConnectWise ScreenConnect

items.title: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours of Disclosure
items.description: In yet another instance of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM Python package has come under active exploitation in the wild within 36 hours of the bug becoming public knowledge. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS score: 9.3), is an SQL injection that could be exploited to modify the underlying

items.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
items.description: The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives.

items.title: NSA Chief During Snowden Affair Shares Regrets, Reflections 13 Years Later
items.description: Chris Inglis was the head civilian in charge at the NSA when the Snowden leaks exploded. He gets candid about mistakes the organization made, and what CISOs need to know about spotting potential threats, media disclosures, and "enculturation."

items.title: Feuding Ransomware Groups Leak Each Other's Data
items.description: When 0APT and KryBit attacked each other, they exposed infrastructure and operational data, giving defenders rare insight into ransomware operations.

items.title: Vidar Rises to Top of Chaotic Infostealer Market
items.description: The malware has filled the gap created by last year's law enforcement takedowns of Lumma and Rhadamanthys.

items.title: Researchers Discover Critical GitHub CVE-2026-3854 RCE Flaw Exploitable via Single Git Push
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security vulnerability impacting GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server that could allow an authenticated user to obtain remote code execution with a single "git push" command. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-3854 (CVSS score: 8.7), is a case of command injection that could allow an attacker with push access to a repository to achieve

items.title: Cyber Command, NSA chief warns foreign adversaries likely to target midterms
items.description: Army Gen. Joshua Rudd told lawmakers “we are postured and ready to support as required or tasked, making sure that we safeguard our elections.”

items.title: Fresh Wave of GlassWorm VS Code Extensions Slices Through Supply Chain
items.description: Attackers continue to scale a campaign to seed Open VSX with seemingly benign VS Code extensions that spread self-propagating malware.

items.title: HTTP Requests with X-Vercel-Set-Bypass-Cookie Header, (Tue, Apr 28th)
items.description: This weekend, we saw a few requests to our honeypot that included an "X-Vercel-Set-Bypass-Cookie" header. A sample request:

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 008 - 26-Day Pause Ends with Three Concurrent Compromises (Checkmarx KICS, Bitwarden CLI Cascade, xinference PyPI), CanisterSprawl npm Worm Identified, and Tier 1 Coverage Returns, (Mon, Apr 27th)
items.description: This update succeeds&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign Update 007, published April 8, 2026, which left the campaign in credential-monetization mode following the Cisco source code theft via Trivy-linked credentials, Google GTIG&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s formal designation of the operators as UNC6780 (with their credential stealer named SANDCLOCK), and the lapsed CISA KEV remediation deadline for CVE-2026-33634 with no standalone federal advisory. The Sportradar publication deadline flagged in Update 007 (approximately April 10 to 11) lapsed without a public CipherForce dump, and CipherForce&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s leak infrastructure has remained offline. Twelve days after Update 007, the technical compromise picture changed sharply across the W17 window (April 20 through April 26).

CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
Apple Patches Exploited Notification Flaw, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
items.description: Apple yesterday released iOS/iPadOS 26.4.2 and iOS/iPadOS 18.7.8. This update fixes a single Notification Services vulnerability, CVE-2026-28950:

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, April 23rd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9904, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
